method: per-sample PAIRED interleaved CUDA-event timing (warmup 30, iters 300), correctness-gated, same model+GPU
headline: After 10 min: AMK 1226us vs cuBLAS-graphed eager 1083us. Drift-robust PAIRED ratio (cuBLAS/AMK) median 0.88x (p10-p90 0.82-0.93); >1 => AMK faster. vs naive eager-perop 4442us => 3.6x faster.

vllm.reason: vLLM has no Windows wheels; not a local baseline.
vllm.paper_reference: vLLM default (cudagraph) on H100 with SmolLM2-135M = 8.65 ms/tok; AMK there = 14.30 ms/tok (1.65x slower) — different model/GPU, see paper Table 8 / h100.json. Not comparable to this small-model run.

honesty: AMK's 10-min result is a correct, measured, drift-robust speedup over AMK's OWN baseline. cuBLAS-graphed eager is the strongest local baseline (library GEMMs + graph replay). The result below is the literal measurement, reported whichever way it falls.
